def: part. (f. -ā), Utterly destroyed, ruined; effaced.

ety: wināś˚ = S. विनाशय (ति), caus. of rt. वि+नश्
def: v.t. To destroy, ruin, spoil, &c. (i.q. bināsnā, q.v.).
v.n. = binasnā, q.v.
v.n. = binasnā, q.v.
s.m. (old H.) = vi-nāś, q.v.
s.m. (old H.) = vi-nāś, q.v.
adj. = vināś-mān, q.v.s.v. vināś.
part. = vinaśyat, q.v.
part. (f. -antī), Being lost or ruined; perishing; decaying.
s.f. Name of one of the wives of Kaśyap and mother of Aruṇ and Garuḍ; — a sort of basket.
part. (f. -ā), Bent down, bowed, stooping, inclined (i.q. binat, q.v.); sunk down, depressed; — curved, crooked; — prostrate; humble, modest; — cast down, depressed, dispirited.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Having no lord or master; unprotected; unowned; deserted; — an unprotected person, &c.
s.f. = binatī, q.v.

s.m. Leading, guidance; training, discipline; refinement, gentlemanly bearing; good behaviour, propriety of conduct, decorum, decency; — modesty, affability, courtesy, humility, mildness; obeisance, reverence (cf. binay)
subs: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Conforming to rules of discipline; compliant, tractable, humble; modest, &c.
or vinay-vān, adj. Possessed of modesty, well-behaved, modest, &c. (i.q. vinayī).

def: s.m. (f. -ikā), A remover (of obstacles); — a Buddh; — a name of the god Ganeś; and of Garuḍ (the bird and vehicle of Vishṇu); — a Guru or spiritual preceptor.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Well-trained, disciplined, &c. (i.q. vi-nīt, q.v.); — a well-behaved person; a modest person, &c.
s.f. The wife of Garuḍ; — the wife of a spiritual preceptor; — a spiritual preceptress.
s.m. = وندو vindu, q.v. (and cf. bind).
adj. & s.m. Finding, getting, gaining, obtaining, acquiring; — gainer, acquirer, &c. (found as the last member of a few compounds, e.g. go-vind, q.v.s.v. go).
s.f. A kind of plant, Annona reticulata; — small cardamoms.
s.m. = next, q.v.

s.m. Name of a range of mountains connecting the northern extremities of the Western and Eastern Ghāṭs, and separating Hindūstān proper from the Dakkhan (it marks the southern boundary of the sacred land of the Hindūs); — a hunter
subs: (˚ya+aṭ˚), s.f. The great Vindhya forest (which appears to have spread, at one time, from near Mathurā to the Narmadā)
(vindhyā = vindhya), s.m. Name of a place of Hindū pilgrimage in the Vindhya mountains; — a triangle
(˚ya+ać˚), or vindhya-parvat, s.m. The Vindhya range of mountains
(˚ya+āv˚), s.f. The wife of the Asur Bali
or vindhyāvalī-sut, s.m. 'Son of Vindhyāvalī,' name of the Asur Vāṇ

s.m., or vindhya-vāsinī, vulg. vindhyā-vāsinī, s.f. Name of Durgā; — a village and temple sacred to Durgā (under the forms of Yog-māyā and Bhog-māyā), situated about three miles from Mirzapur on the Ganges (it is a place of great resort).

def: s.m. = S وندهياٿوي विन्ध्याटवी vindhyāṭavī, s.f. See s.v. vindhya.
s.f. = S وندهياچل विन्ध्याचल vindhyāćal, s.m. See s.v. vindhya.

s.m. A drop (of water or other liquid); a point, dot, spot, mark, speck (i.q. bindu, q.v.); the nasal dot anusvār; — semen; — the mark of a tooth or a bite; — a mark of colouring matter on an elephant's face or trunk; — the part of the forehead between the eyebrows
subs: s.m. Name of a king (a son of Ćandra-gupt)
s.m. Name of a sacred lake (which is a place of Hindū pilgrimage)
s.m. Name of a king (a son of Kshatraujas).

def: s.m. A drop (of water, &c.; — i.q. vindu, q.v.).
pron. = unheṅ, dat. & acc. pl. of wah, q.v.
s.m. Exchange, barter; substitution; requital, retaliation (syn. badlā); — transmutation (of letters); — a pledge; deposit; security.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ikā), Reproving, reprehending, reviling; blaspheming; — reprover, &c.
part. (f. -ā), Reproached, blamed, reprehended; reviled, abused; blasphemed.
s.m. Falling down, falling, fall; — a great fall, ruin; unavoidable evil, misfortune, calamity; destruction; decay; death; — pain, distress; — disrespect
subs: adj. Announcing misfortune or destruction, portentous.

def: adj. = niśćint, q.v.
part. (f. -ā), Ascertained, determined, settled, decided, certain.

part. (f. -ā), Well-trained, educated, disciplined, well-controlled; — chastened; tamed; broken in; — refined, well-behaved; decorous, decent; — compliant, governable, tractable; — modest; virtuous; demure; meek, gentle, placid
subs: (˚ta+āt˚), adj. & s.m.f. Havin a well-controlled mind; well-behaved; of a humble or tractable disposition, humble, lowly; — a well-behaved person, &c.

def: s.f. Training, discipline; refinement, good behaviour; reverence, obeisance, homage.
s.m. Driving away, removing, dismissing, abandoning, &c. (i.q. binod, q.v.).
adj. Twenty; — s.f. A score
s.m. The tree Echites scholaris.

s.m. Putting down, placing, laying down; depositing; entrusting; a deposit; — orderly arrangement, disposition, adjustment; — a site, or a receptacle (on or in which anything is placed or deposited); — collecting; collection, assemblage.
s.m. = وپکشي vi-pakshī, q.v.
s.f. The base of vipat, q.v.; and the form euphonically assumed by vipat in comp. before words beginning with u, g, &c., e.g. vipad-uddhār, vipad-grast, &c., for which see vipat.
s.f. = vipad = vipat, q.v.

s.m. Cooking thoroughly, dressing; ripening, maturing; — ripeness, maturity; — concoction (of food), digestion; change of state (in general); — unexpected consequence (of actions); unexpected event or occurrence, improbable result; — the consequence or result of any action (either in this or in a former birth) matured by the operation of time; — calamity, distress, poverty, difficulty, embarrassment; — flavour, taste.

adj. Being on a differeut or opposite side, opposed, contrary (to), adverse, hostile, inimical; — s.m. An opponent, an adversary, a rival, an enemy; — a disputant; — (in Gram.) an exception; — (in Logic) a negative instance; an instance on the opposite side
subs: adj. Impartial, indifferent; — s.m. Impartiality, indifference.

def: s.f. = S وپکشتو विपक्षत्व vi-pakshatva, s.m. Contrariety, opposition, contradiction, controversy, hostility, enmity.
s.m. = S وپکشتا विपक्षता vi-pakshatā, s.f. Contrariety, opposition, contradiction, controversy, hostility, enmity.
adj. & s.m. = vi-paksh, q.v.
s.m. A moment, an instant; a simple breathing; (in Astron.) a second.
s.f. Adverse circumstances; mischief; rain.

ety: rt. i with pari and vi
def: part. (f. -ā) & postpn. Turned the wrong way, reversed, inverted; inverse, converse, opposite, adverse, perverse, contrary, repugnant; unfavourable, inauspicious; — contrary to what is right; having a contrary disposition; changed for the worse, disordered; gone wrong or astray; — different (to, -ke), contrariwise (to); otherwise (than), differently (from, -se).
s.f. = S وپريتتو विपरीतत्व vi-parītava, s.m. Inversion; reverse or opposite state or condition; contrariness, contrariety, perverseness, &c.
s.m. = S وپريتتا विपरीतता vi-parītatā, s.f. Inversion; reverse or opposite state or condition; contrariness, contrariety, perverseness, &c.

r: vi-paryās, as, pari, vi, vi-paryāy, i, vi-paryay
def: (rt. as with pari and vi), s.m. = S وپريت विपर्याय vi-paryāy (rt. i with pari and vi), s.m. = وپريي vi-paryay, q.v.

s.m. Reverse; inversion; contrariety, perverseness, opposition, repugnance; enmity, hostility; — change, interchange, exchange; — change of purpose or conduct; — morbid change; reverse of fortune, calamity, misfortune, adverse fate; — error, misapprehension, mistake; failure of conception; — transgression, trespass.

r: vi-paryāy, i, pari, vi, vi-paryās, as, vi-paryay
def: (rt. i with pari and vi), s.m. = S وپرياس विपर्यास vi-paryās (rt. as with pari and vi), s.m. = وپريي vi-paryay, q.v.

s.f. The river Beas (one of the five rivers of the Panjāb; said to be so called as having destroyed the cord which the Muni Vasishṭha had tied round his neck to hang himself through grief for the death of his son slain by Viśvā-mitra; this river is identified with the Hyphasis of Arrian, the Greek name being a corruption of Bipasha).

s.f. The river Beas (one of the five rivers of the Panjāb; said to be so called as having destroyed the cord which the Muni Vasishṭha had tied round his neck to hang himself through grief for the death of his son slain by Viśvā-mitra; this river is identified with the Hyphasis of Arrian, the Greek name being a corruption of Bipasha).

r: vi-pat, bipat, vipad, vi-patti, vi-pati, bipati
def: s.f. Adversity, calamity, misfortune, mishap, disaster, distress; failing; — pain, agony; death, dying
subs: s.m. Deliverance or extrication from misfortune
or vipatti-rahit, adj. Free from misfortune; prosperous, happy
s.m. An ocean of misfortunes; heavy calamity
s.m. Time of calamity or misfortune, season of distress, adversity
adj. (f. -ā), Seized or devoured by misfortune, overtaken by calamity, involved in disaster or misfortune, in difficulties
s.m. Cause or occasion of trouble
or vipatti-yukt, adj. (f. -ā), Attended with misfortune, calamitous, unfortunate, unhappy.

def: s.f. The river Beas (one of the five rivers of the Panjāb; said to be so called as having destroyed the cord which the Muni Vasishṭha had tied round his neck to hang himself through grief for the death of his son slain by Viśvā-mitra; this river is identified with the Hyphasis of Arrian, the Greek name being a corruption of Bipasha).
s.f. corr. of vipat, or of vipadā, q.v.
s.m. A different path; a by-path; — a wrong way, a bad road; wrong course, evil conduct.
adj. (f. -ā), Fruitless, barren, unproductive; unprofitable; ineffectual, bootless, vain, idle, useless; unmeaning.
s.f. = S وپهلتو विफलत्व vi-phalatva, s.m. Fruitlessn ess, barrenness, unproductiveness; unprofitableness, uselessness, &c.
s.m. = S وپهلتا विफलता vi-phalatā, s.f. Fruitlessn ess, barrenness, unproductiveness; unprofitableness, uselessness, &c.
s.m. A wood, forest, thicket, grove.
s.m. (old H.) = وپر vipra, or vipr, q.v.
s.m. An utterer of praise, a singer of hymns; a Rishi; a praiser; a poet; — a Brāhmaṇ, a priest; — a sage, seer, wise man
subs: s.m. The sacred feet of a Brāhman
vulg. bipar-badh, s.m. The murder of a Brāhmaṇ.

ety: rt. pad with prati and vi
def: s.f. Going in different or opposite directions; perplexity, confusion; — difference, opposition (of opinion, or interests, &c.), mutual contrariety, contradiction, discrepancy; contest, dispute; conflict (of evidence); dissent, objection (in argument); — various acquirements, conversancy; mutual connexion or relation.
adj. Large, ample, great, extensive, broad, wide, spacious, roomy, capacious; abundant; deep, profound; — s.m. (f. -ā), A respectable man; — the mountain Meru; — the Himalaya range.
s.f. A respectable woman; — the earth.
s.f. = S وپلتو विपुलत्व vi-pulatva, s.m. Largeness, greatness, magnitude, extent, width, spaciousness.
s.m. = S وپلتا विपुलता vi-pulatā, s.f. Largeness, greatness, magnitude, extent, width, spaciousness.

adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Heroic, brave, valiant; mighty, powerful, strong; eminent, excellent; — a hero, &c. (i.q. bīr, q.v.); — an actor, a mime; — the heroic sentiment, heroism; — fire; sacrificial fire
subs: s.m. A distinguished hero; — a horse fit for the aśva-medh, sacrifice
s.m. Name of a king of Gūrjar; — a proper name (of men)
(lit. 'having an army of heroes'), s.m. Name of the father of Nala
s.f. Hero-worship. (For other compounds see bīr.)

def: s.f. (m. vīra or vīr), The wife of a hero; — a heroine; — a wife and mother, a matron; — the plantain tree; — name of several plants.
s.m. Arranging, arrangement; — constructing; contriving, contrivance; making; compiling, composing; composition, compilation; — embellishing; embellishment; any artificial or ornamental fabric.
s.f. = vi-raćan, q.v.
part. (fem. -ā), Arranged; constructed; formed, made, prepared; contrived; effected; — written, compiled, composed; — put in, inlaid, set; — embellished, ornamented; furnished (with).
v.t. To arrange; to construct, contrive; to make, &c. (see viraćan, and biraćnā).

s.m. (dialec.) Fame, glory, &c. (i.q. birad, q.v.); — the dress of heroes; military uniform or accoutrements, the habiliments of war; — a badge betokening some excellence or superiority; — a badge of office (cf. wardī, and urdī).
s.m. Opposition; prevention; — vexation, annoyance; — a kind of Rākshas.
s.m. Absence of desire or passion, indifference (to), disinclination, aversion (to), disregard of all sensual enjoyment; stoicism.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Void of passion or desire; entertaining aversion or dislike; stoical; — an ascetic; a stoical person.
s.m. Separation, parting, absence, &c. (see birah); — pain arising from separation; — loneliness; distance; — relinquishment; abandonment, desertion; — cessation
subs: (˚ha+ag˚), s.f. 'The fire of separation,' the agony or disconsolateness of parted lovers
or virah-want, adj. (f. -vatī) = virahī, q.v. (For other compounds, see birah.)

def: s.m. = birhā.

adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Separated (from), absent, or apart (from another); lonely, solitary, deserted; — suffering the pangs of absence from a beloved one; love-sick; amorous; sensual; — one who is suffering the pangs of absence from a beloved one, &c. (see birahī, or birhī, the pop. forms).
adj. & s.m. = vi-rahī, q.v.
s.f. = vi-rahiṇī, and birahan, qq.v.
s.f. (of vi-rahī), A woman separated from her husband, or suffering the pangs of absence from her lover (i.q. virahin, and birahan, q.v.); — wages, hire.

part. (f. -ā), Separated (from), retired or withdrawn from company; abandoned, deserted, forsaken, relinquished, left, left alone; solitary, lonely; — devoid (of), exempt (from), free (from); bereft (of), destitute (of).

ety: Prk. विरहियो, and विरहिआ; S. विरह+इकः, and इका
def: adj. & s.m. & f. Love-sick, &c. (i.q. virahī, and birahī and birhiyā).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Free from dust; — devoid of passion, passionless; — one who is free from passion.

s.m. Splendour, beauty; display; — a man of the regal or military class; — the first progeny of Brahmā; — (in the Vedānta phil.) the consciousness which perceives collections or aggregates; — the body.
s.f. A species of grass (i.q. dūrvā, or dūb); — name of a river.
adj. Shining, brilliant, splendid, handsome, gorgeous.
part. Shining, &c. (i.q. virājmān, q.v.).
part. (f. -ā), Made splendid, illuminated, irradiated; splendid; displayed, made visible, manifested.

ety: wirāj˚ = S. विराज(ति), rt: राज् with वि
def: v.t. & v.n. = birājnā, q.v.

part. (f. -ā), Changed in disposition or feeling, disaffected, estranged, alienated; averse (to); disgusted (with), wearied (of); indifferent (to); disinclined; dégagé; — inattentive; — free from passion or affection; void of attachment to worldly objects (cf. the pop. forms birakt, birkat); — feeling excessive passion (for), impassioned; attached (to), fond (of), pleased (with); — s.m. One who is free from passion, &c.; — a kind of ascetic who has renounced the world
subs: adj. Having the heart or affections estranged, disinclined (to), averse (to), disliking; — s.m. Disinclination, aversion, &c.

def: s.f. = S ورکت विरक्ति vi-rakti, s.f. Change of disposition or feeling, disaffection, estrangement, alienation; disinclination, indifference (to); inattention; aversion, disgust; — absence of attachment or affection (for), freedom from passion; weanedness from the world.

s.f. = S ورکتتا विरक्तता vi-raktatā, s.f. Change of disposition or feeling, disaffection, estrangement, alienation; disinclination, indifference (to); inattention; aversion, disgust; — absence of attachment or affection (for), freedom from passion; weanedness from the world.
s.m. = وڐال viḍāl, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Having interstices, separated by intervals (whether of space or time); not close or compact, &c. (see biral).
s.m. = viḍāl, q.v.
adj. & s.m. See the form birlā.

s.m. Leaving off, cessation, rest, repose, discontinuance (of working, speaking, &c.); stopping, stop, pause, stoppage of sound; (in Gram.) 'the stop,' name of a small oblique stroke placed under a consonant to denote that it is quiescent, i.e. that it has no vowel inherent or otherwise pronounced after it (e.g. विराज् virāj); — adj. Uneasy, unquiet, agitated.
s.m. A fragrant grass used in certain religious ceremonies, Andropogon aromaticum or muricatum.
adj. Laid waste, depopulated, ruined; waste, desolate, desert; dreary, dismal
subs: v.t. To lay waste, ruin, destroy, depopulate, desolate
s.m. The deserted site of a village.

def: s.m. A desolate place; a place full of ruins; a solitude; — a waste; forest-land.
s.m. A name of Brahmā; — a Brāhmaṇ.
adv. In a variety of ways, in different ways.
s.f. Desolation, depopulation, destruction, ruin, dilapidation; desert place
adj. (f. -ā), Sapless; — flavourless, tasteless, insipid, vapid; — ill-flavoured; nauseous; — painful.

ety: for A. وراثة, inf. n. of ورث 'to receive by inheritance'
def: s.f. Inheritance, heritage, patrimony; heirship; hereditary right
subs: adv. By inheritance, as an heir-Ioom
s.m. Deed of inheritance or heirship.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian وِرَاثَت (wirāsat), from Arabic وِرَاثَة (wirāṯa).

ety: for A. وراثةً, acc. of wirās̤at
def: adv. By way of inheritance, as an heirloom

s.m. Name of one of the midland or north-west districts of India (supposed to be the modern Berār); — name of an ancient king (the Pāṇḍavas being obliged to live in concealment during the thirteenth year of their exile, journeyed to the court of this king and entered his service in various disguises)
subs: vulg. virāṭaj, s.m. An inferior sort of diamond (said to be found in the country Virāṭ).

def: s.m. Splendour, beauty; — the first progeny of Brahmā; the producer of the first Manu or Svāyambhuva (who was the secondary framer of the visible universe); — ʻruler of the various forms of bodies,' a name of Intellect located in and ruling over the collective aggregate of bodies; embodied spirit
subs: or virāṭ-deh or dehī (prop. virāḍ-deh, &c.), or virāṭ-rūp (prop. virāḍ-rūp), or virāṭ-svarūp, adj. 'Having the body of Virāṭ,' or 'consisting of Virāj,' epithets of the Supreme Being (as expanded and explicated in the creation, or as developed and exhibited in the radiance and glory of the Universal system); — monstrous, huge, enormous, gigantic.

def: s.f. Heroism, valour, prowess; boldness, manliness.
part. (f. -ā), Ceased, stopped; desisted (from); rested; ended, concluded.
s.m. See s.v. virāṭ.
adj. Having no car or chariot, chariotless.
s.f. Cessation, discontinuance, stop, rest, pause, end, term; — indifference.
s.m. = vīratā, q.v.

ety: v.n. fr. ورد 'to enter; to approach,' &c.
def: s.m. Approach, access; — continual motion or employment, incessant labour; daily use; habitual practice; practice, habit; — a task; a self-imposed daily task or service; — a portion or section of the Qorʼān (fixed for reading at a certain time); — a halting-place, a stage
subs: s.m.(?) Speaking perpetually; fluency of speech; — adv. Ready on the tongue, got by rote
v.n. To be ready on the tongue; to be got by rote or by heart
v.t. To repeat; — to make a practice or habit of
s.m. A portion of the Qorʼān fixed for reading at a certain time; daily devotions
To perform one's daily task of devotion, &c.

def: adj. Emptying, purging; purgative, aperient, cathartic (i.q. rećak).
s.m. Evacuation (of the bowels), purging, diarrhœa; — a purgative.
s.m. = vi-rećan, q.v.

s.m. Opposition; obstruction, hindrance, impediment; restraint, check; — surrounding, investing; siege, blockade; confinement; — (in Logic) contradiction; contrariety; antithesis; contrast; inconsistency; — difference, variance, antagonism, hostility, enmity, animosity, quarrel, contention, litigation, dispute, war, strife; — calamity, misfortune
subs: (˚dha+āć˚), vulg. virodh-ćārī, adj. & s.m. = virodhī, q.v.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Opposing, hindering, preventing, obstructing; counteracting; — blockading, besieging; — obstructive; exclusive; contradictory, inconsistent; — antagonistic, adverse, inimical; hostile; — disputatious, litigious, contentious, quarrelsome; — an opponent, antagonist, enemy; — obstructer, &c.; — a quarrelsome person.
s.f. Fixed rule, institute, ordinance.

ety: inf. n. of ورث 'to receive by inheritance'
def: s.m. Receiving by inheritance, succeeding as heir.
s.m. Inheritance, heritage; a bequest (i.q. wirās̤at, q.v.)
subs: (-ko), To acquire (a property) by inheritance or bequest
v.n. To descend or come to (one) as a heritage; to proceed from an ancestor.

ety: wirud or wirad, q.v.+ait = āʼit = Prk. आइंतो or आइत्तो = आयंतो = आवंतो or आवेंतो = S. आपयन् (the aff. of the pres. part. with the caus. aug. āpi)
def: s.m. (dialec.) A man in military uniform, an accoutred warrior; — one wearing a badge of distinction, or of office.

part. (f. -ā) & postpn. Opposed, hindered, checked, restrained, arrested, obstructed, &c.; thwarted (by); prohibited, forbidden; excluded, disqualified; — opposite (to), the opposite (of), contrary (to), against, averse (to); at variance (with); hostile (to), adverse; discordant; incongruous, inconsistent; unpropitious, unfavourable; — contrariwise (as a part. viruddh governs the ablative; as a postp., the genitive, -ke).
s.f. (m. -tva), Opposition, contrariety; perverseness; incongruity; — aversion; hostility, enmity; disagreeableness.
adj. Free from pain or sickness, &c.; in health, well, &c. (syn. nirogī).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Misshapen, deformed, &c. (i.q. birūp, q.v.).
s.f. A deformed or hideous woman, a female monstrosity; — name of the wife of Yam.
adj. (f. -ikā) = vi-rūp, q.v.
adj. = vi-rūp, q.v.
s.m. Vigour, strength, power; heroism, prowess, valour, courage, fortitude, firmness; — virile energy, virility; dignity, consequence; — splendour, lustre; — semen virile; seed (of plants, &c.)
subs: s.m. 'Seed-gift' (when a husband of one of the three subordinate castes is either dead or away in a distant part of the country, and the wife has not as yet borne a male child, the purohit of that household temporarily fills the husband's place and begets a son)
adj. (f. -vatī), Possessing vigour, vigorous, strong, stout, robust; energetic; efficacious; — heroic; illustrious; victorious
adj. (f. -ā), Devoid of courage, or valour, or prowess, or vigour, &c.
adj. (f. -ā), Possessed of courage, or vigour, &c.
s.f. Loss of courage, or vigour, &c.; loss of virile energy, impotence
adj. (f. -ā), Destitute of power or vigour, &c., deprived of strength; cowardly; impotent; seedless.

def: s.m. The film or fibres of the stalk of the water-lily or lotus.
(Braj) = us, obl. or format. of the pron. wah, q.v.
adj. corr. of وشد viśad, q.v.
adj. Clean, clear, pure, pellucid; — white; — pure, spotless; — lucid, perspicuous; — evident, apparent, obvious, manifest; — beautiful
subs: s.f. A clear and beautiful voice; clear or perspicuous speech; chaste speech, eloquence (i.q. bisad-bānī).

def: s.m. A ligature, a vinculum, a tie, fastening; — (inf. n. of واثق 'to enter into a compact (with),' &c., iii of وثق), s.m. Entering into a compact or confederacy (with); stipulating; pledging (one's) faith.
s.m. The plant Euphorbia (lit. 'filled with fibres').

s.f. Name of the sixteenth Nakshatra or lunar asterism (figured by a decorated gateway and arch and containing four (or originally two) stars: it is probably to be connected with the quadrangle of stars ι, α, β, γ Libræ; — it is fabled to be jealous of the partiality of the Moon for Roninl, q.v.).
s.f. corr. of وشاکها vi-śākhā, q.v. (and see bisākhā).
adj. (f. -ā), Large, great, wide, broad, extensive, widely spread; — great, eminent, illustrious (cf. bisāl).

ety: inf. n. of واصل, iii of وصل 'to be united in friendship,' &c.
def: s.m. Meeting, interview; union, conjunction; arrival (at), attainment; fruition, enjoyment of any desired object; (in the language of Sūfīs) dying, death; — society, confederacy.
ex:
وہ فراق اور وہ وصال کہاں
وہ شب و روز و ماہ و سال کہاں
voh firāq aur voh visāl kahā̃

that separation and that reunion, where?
those nights and days and months and years, where?

def: adj. corr. of وشال viśāl, q.v. (and see bisāl).
s.f. = S وشالتو विशालत्व viśālatva, s.m. Largeness, greatness, width, breadth, extension, expansion, magnitude, bulk; — eminence, distinction.
s.m. = S وشالتا विशालता viśālatā, s.f. Largeness, greatness, width, breadth, extension, expansion, magnitude, bulk; — eminence, distinction.
s.m. See بسن bisan.
adj. (f. -ā), Fearless, undaunted; safe (cf. ni-sank, or nis-sank).
adj. Learned, wise; skilful, skilled (in), conversant (with), — famous, celebrated, eminent; — confident, bold, presuming, daring.
s.m. See the pop. form bisarg.
s.m. Leaving, quitting, relinquishing, &c. (i.q. bisarjan, q.v.).
ex: the practice of immersing an idol of Ganesh in the water on Anant Chaturdashi

def: adj. (f. -ā), Weaponless, unarmed; disarmed.

ety: for A. وصاية, v.n. fr. وصی; see waṣīyat
def: s.f. A commandment, mandate; testament, will.
pron. = ase, dat. & acc. sing. of wah, q.v. (cf. wis).
s.m. &c., corr. of وشيش vi-śesh, q.v.
adj. & adv. See بشيش biśesh
subs: s.f. Reflecting on or perceiving differences; — a particular or special thought; — (in Math.) a particular operation in extracting roots, composition by the difference of the products
adj. (f. -vatī), Possessed of some distinguishing property; peculiar; excellent; superior, better.

def: adj. Distinguishing, discriminative, characteristic; attributive.
adj. & s.m. See بشيشن biśeshaṇ.
adj. & s.m. = H وسيشيه विसेष्य vi-seshya, adj. corr. of vi-śeshaṇ, and vi-śeshya, qq.v.
s.f. Peculiarity, speciality; — abundance, plenty.
adj. = viśeshak, q.v.
part. (f. -ā), Distinguished, made different or distinct; defined; separated, divided; — having as an attribute; predicated; — excellent, superior.

part. adj. (f. -ā), To be distinguished or discriminated; determinable; requiring to be defined or determined; — distinguished, pre-eminent; principal, primary, chief; — s.m. (in Gram.) The word to be distinguished, the thing or object to be particularized or defined by another word (which other word is called viśeshaṇ, q.v.), a substantive; the subject or object of a predicate
subs: adj. 'Subservient or subject to the viśeshya or substantive,' that must agree with its substantive in gender, number, &c.; — dependent (on), governed (by); — s.m. An adjective.

def: adj. = H وسيشن विसेषण viseshaṇ, adj. & s.m. corr. of vi-śeshaṇ, and vi-śeshya, qq.v.
s.f. Fæces, ordure, excrement.
s.m. Poison, venom, bane, &c. (see bis, the pop. form); — water; — the film or fibres attached to the stalk of the lotus or water-lily; — gum-myrrh
subs: (˚sha+ap˚), adj. (f. -ā), Poison-repelling, antidotic; — s.m. An antidote
s.f. A kind of birth-wort, Aristolochia indica
s.f. The marking-nut plant, Semecarpus anacardium
vulg. bis-bharā, adj. (f. -ī), 'Full of poison,' poisonous, venomous
s.m. The act of eating poison; taking poison
vulg. vishad, adj. (f. -ā), Yielding or giving poison; poisonous; — shedding water; — s.m. A cloud; — green vitriol
vulg. bis-dhar, adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Holding or containing poison, poisonous, venomous; — a snake
s.m. 'Poison-indicator'; the Greek partridge, Perdix rufa (syn. ćakor)
vulg. bis-kapra, s.m. = vish-khaprā, or bis-khaprā, q.v.s.v. bis
s.m. (f. -ī), A kind of insect that lives in (and subsists on) poison
adj. & adv. (f. -ā), Having taken poison; — under the influence of poison
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Poison-destroying, poison-counteracting, antidotal; — an antidote; — the tree Mimosa sirissa
s.m. The plant Arum cucullatum or cordifolium
s.m. A snake-charmer, snake-catcher; one who cures the bite of snakes; — a charm for curing snake-bites
vulg. bis-nāsak, adj. (f. -ikā), Poison-destroying, antidotal; — s.m. An antidote
and vish-nāśī, adj. & s.m. = vish-nāśak (syn. vish-ghātī)
adj. Like poison; — possessing poison, poisonous
vulg. bis-baṭī, s.f. A pill or bolus of poison
s.f. 'Poison-science,' the administration of antidotes; the cure of poisons by drugs or charms
s.m. 'Poison doctor,' a dealer in antidotes; one who professes to cure snake-bites
adj. (f. -ī), Removing venom, antidotal; s.m. An antidote
s.f. The goddess who protects from the venom of snakes; the sister of Vāsuki and wife of Jarat-kāru and goddess of the serpent-race
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), 'Poison-hearted,' cherishing hatred or hostility, malicious, malignant; — a person who cherishes hatred, &c. (For other compounds see bis.)

def: s.m. Dejection, depression, lowness of spirits, despondency, despair; disappointment; — want of energy, languor, lassitude, fatigue, exhaustion; weakness; — sadness, sorrow, affliction; — fear; — dulness; insensibility.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. vish.
adj. (f. -inī), Dejected; disconsolate; sad, sorrowful; — causing dejection, or sadness, &c.; distressing.
adj. (f. -ī), See بسہا bisahā.
adj. (f. -ī) = vishālu, q.v.
adj. Poisonous, venomous.

adj. (f. -ā), Uneven, rough, rugged; difficult of access; unequal, irregular; not even, odd (number); — different; — difficult, hard to be understood; — disagreeable, painful, afflictive, troublesome, vexatious; cross, rough, rude; — odd, unequalled, unparalleled; — intermittent, inconstant; — unfair, dishonest, partial; — bad, adverse, unpropitious; — wicked; — fearful, awful; unhappy; — s.m. Unevenness, inequality; oddness (of numbers); — an inaccessible place; a precipice; a thicket; a pit; — difficulty; pain; misfortune
subs: vulg. bisam-jar, s.m. Irregular fever, irregularly remittent fever; a high or violent fever
s.m. Unequal four-sided figure, quadrilateral, trapezium
s.m. Irregular boldness, temerity, rashness, daring
s.m. 'An unequal-angled oblong figure,' a parallelogram; rhomboid
s.m. A species of metre in which the four feet are dissimilar.

def: s.f. Unevenness, &c. (i.q. visham, q.v.).
s.m. Horn (of an animal); — tusk (of an elephant or of a boar); — a sort of medicinal plant, Costus speciosus.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Having horns, horned; having huge tusks; — any horned animal; an animal with tusks; — a bull; an elephant.
s.f. A horn, &c. (i.q. vishāṇ); — name of a plant (the fruit of which is likened to a ram's horn).
s.m. A species of snake.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. vish.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. vish.

s.m. An object of sense (these are five in number, each of the five organs of sense having its proper vishay or object); — a symbolical expression for the number five; — anything perceptible by the senses, any object (affection, or desire, or art, &c.); — object of concern or attention; worldly object or aim, pursuit, affair, concern, matter, thing; business, transaction
subs: pleasure of sense; sensuality; lust; — subject, matter, topic; sense; region, department, province, sphere, &c.; — (S. vishaye), postpn. In the matter (of, -ke), in respect (of), in regard (to), in relation (to), with reference (to), concerning, regarding, about (syn. bābat)
In this matter, in this respect
(˚ya+ās˚), adj. (f. -ā), Attached to objects of sense, devoted to the world, worldly
s.m. The enjoyment of material or sensual objects; worldly enjoyment, pleasure.

def: adj. Relating to an object; (as last member of compounds) having for an object or subject, relating to, having reference to, on the subject of, treating of, concerning.
adj. See s.v. vishay.

adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), Relating to objects of sense; attached to sensual objects; engaged or interested in worldly occupations or enjoyments; sensual, carnal, worldly; — a king (as engaged in the business of government); a person engaged in worldly pursuits, a man of business; a man of the world, a wordling; a sensualist, voluptuary; — an epithet of Kām-dev, the god of love; — one who disbelieves or denies the existence of everything except objects of sense, a materialist.

ety: S. विष+elā = Prk. इल्लओ = S. इल+कः
def: adj. & s.m. See biselā or bisailā.
adj. (f. -iṇī), Poisonous, venomous.
adj. (rustic) = vishelā, q.v. (cf. bisyar; bisailā).
s.m. corr. of viśva-karmā, q.v.s.v. وشوه viśva.

s.m. Name of one of the principal Hindū deities (in the later mythology regarded as 'the preserver,' and with Brahmā 'the creator' and Śiva 'the destroyer,' constituting the well-known tri-mūrti or triad; the various incarnations (avatār) are considered to be manifestations of this deity); — a pure or pious man; — a name of Agni; and of one of the Vasus; and of an ancient law-giver
subs: s.m. 'Station or step of Vishṇu'; the sky, heaven; the atmosphere; — the sea of milk; — a lotus; — (S. vishṇu+padya), a particular song in the name of Vishṇu

s.f. One of the twelve sankrāntis or sun's passages into a sign of the zodiac (esp. the first sign after the equinox); — the sun's entrance into Taurus, Leo, Scorpio, or Aquarius; — a name of the Ganges (as issuing from the foot of Vishṇu)
s.m. Name of one of the most celebrated of the eighteen Purāṇas
or prīti, s.f. Land granted rent-free to Brāhmaṇs in honour of Vishṇu or to maintain his worship (i.q. vishṇūttar)
s.m. Name of a song in the name of Vishṇu (i.q. vishṇu-pad)
s.m. The holder of a vishṇu-prīt, a class of Brāhmaṇs to whom have been assigned grants of land in the name of Vishṇu
s.m. Name of a sect of mendicants who beg in the name of Vishṇu
s.m. Name of a leader of the sect of Bhaktas; — name of the author of the Panć-tantra; and of a certain scribe
adj. (f. -ī), Emanated from Vishṇu; permeated by Vishṇu
(˚nu+ut˚), s.m. A grant of rent-free lands to Brāhmaṇs in honour of Vishṇu or to maintain his worship (i.q. vishṇu-prīti)
s.f. The plant Echites caryophyllata (lit. 'beloved by Vishṇu'); — a small fragrant shrub, Ocymum sanctum (syn. tulsī).

def: adj. & s.m. = vaishṇava, q.v.
s.f. Ordure, fæces, excrement (cf. bīṭh or bīṭ).
s.m. Stopping, staying; — obstruction, impediment, hindrance, obstacle; — obstruction of the urine or fæces, ischury, constipation; — paralysis, loss of motion.
s.m. Anything spread out; a layer, bed; a couch; seat, chair, stool, &c.; — a handful of Kuśa grass; a seat made of a sheaf of such grass for the Brāhmaṇ who presides at a sacrifice; — a tree.
s.f. corr. of vishṭā, q.v.
s.f. corr. of next, q.v.

s.f. Working, labouring; labour, occupation; act, action; — hire, wages; — unpaid labour; — sending, despatching; — consigning to hell; — (rt. viś) the seventh of the variable astronomical periods called Karaṇas.
s.m. = وشو vishuv, q.v.
s.m. The equinoctial point or equinox
subs: s.f. The shadow of the gnomon or index of a dial at noon when the sun is on the equinoctial points
s.m. The day of the equinox
or vishu-vat-rekhā, s.f. The equinoctial line
or vishu-vat-sankrānti, s.f. The sun's equinoctial passage, the passing of the sun into the next sign at either equinox
s.m. The time of the equinox.

def: s.m. The equinox (i.q. vishuv)
s.m. An arrow; — an iron crow.
s.f. A minute arrow; — a sort of pin, or needle; — a spade; a hoe; — a spindle; — a highway.
adj. corr. of وششٿ viśishṭ, q.v.

part. adj. (f. -ā), Distinguished (by), endowed (with), invested (with), possessed (of), having; — having distinctive qualities or exclusive properties; — distinguished, distinct, particular, especial, special, peculiar; — superior, excellent, pre-eminent, choice, best; respectable.
s.f. = S وششٿتو विशिष्टत्व vi-śishṭatva, s.m. The having distinguishing or characteristic qualities; distinction, individuality, speciality, peculiarity; — excellence, superiority, eminence.
s.m. = S وششٿتا विशिष्टता vi-śishṭatā, s.f. The having distinguishing or characteristic qualities; distinction, individuality, speciality, peculiarity; — excellence, superiority, eminence.
s.m. corr. of وشليش vi-ślesh, q.v.
s.m. Disunion, disjunction, separation; — difference; — distance; absence; bereavement; — a chasm.
s.m. Forgetting; obliviousness.
s.m. Wonder, astonishment, surprise, amazement, admiration; — disconcertedness; dismay; — pride, arrogance; doubt, uncertainty, perplexity
subs: or vismayānvit (˚ya+āp˚), adj. Filled with wonder, astonished, surprised, &c. (i.q. vi-smit and vi-smayī).

def: adj. Astonished, surprised, &c. (i.q. vi-smit, q.v.; and cf. bismayī).
part. (f. -ā), Astonished, surprised, amazed, wonderstruck, astounded, dismayed; disconcerted; perplexed.
part. (f. -ā), Forgotten, passed out of the memory or recollection.
s.f. Forgetting; forgetfulness; loss of memory; obliviousness.
s.m. corr. of विष्णु vishṇu, q.v.
adj. Freed from grief, free from sorrow, happy.
adj. (f. -ā), Clear, apparent, manifest, evident, distinct, open; plain, intelligible; — adv. Clearly, evidently, &c.
s.m. = S وسپهوٿا विस्फोटा vi-sphoṭā, s.f. = S وسپهوٿك वीस्फोटक vī-sphoṭak, s.m. A boil, tumour; pustule; — small-pox.
s.f. = S وسپهوٿ विस्फोट vi-sphoṭ, s.m. = S وسپهوٿك वीस्फोटक vī-sphoṭak, s.m. A boil, tumour; pustule; — small-pox.
s.m. = S وسپهوٿ विस्फोट vi-sphoṭ, s.m. = S وسپهوٿا विस्फोटा vi-sphoṭā, s.f. A boil, tumour; pustule; — small-pox.
s.f. A substance smelling like raw meat (syn. hapushā).
s.m. A smell like that of raw meat.
s.m. = وشرام vi-śrām, q.v. (and see bisrām).
s.m. Rest, repose, &c. (see bisrām, and vi-śrānti; syn. farāg̠at)
subs: s.m. Name of a ghāṭ or landing-place near Mathurā (so called from Krishṇ having rested there on one occasion)
s.m. Day of rest, the Sabbath.

def: v.t. = bisrānā, q.v.
part. corr. of وشرانت vi-śrānt, q.v. (and cf. bisrānt).
part. (f. -ā), Rested, reposed; reposing; — ceased; ceasing, desisting (from); — composed, calm.
s.f. Rest, repose, quiet, ease; cessation from toil or fatigue, remission of work, relaxation; pause, stop; — ease, composure, tranquillity.
adj. (f. -ā), Of or relating to rest; imparting rest, affording repose or relaxation, &c.

s.m. Spreading, extension, expansion; — prolixity, diffuseness, detailed description, minute detail; — anything spread out, a layer; a bed (cf. bistar); a seat; — abundance, quantity; multitude, assemblage, a large company.

s.m. Spreading; spread, extension, expansion, diffusion, development (i.q. bistār); — breadth, amplitude; vastness, expanse; — copiousness, abundance; — particularizing at length, detail, amplification, prolixity; — diameter (of a circle); — the branch of a tree with its new shoots; a shrub
subs: adv. In detail, at length, fully, &c.

def: adj. (f. -ikā) = vi-stārī, q.v.
adj. Spreading, extending, enlarging, expanding, amplifying; dwelling upon at length; explaining in detail; — large, ample; diffuse, prolix (see bistārī).
part. (f. -ā) = vi-stīrṇ, q.v.

ety: wistār˚ = S. विस्तारय (ति), caus. of rt. वि+स्तृ
def: v.t. To spread, extend, enlarge, expand, diffuse, develop, &c. (i.q. bistārnā; vistār (or bistār) karnā; see vistār).
part. (f. -ā), Spread, spread out; extended, expanded; enlarged; diffused; — amplified, fully stated; — ample, broad, wide; large, great; capacious, roomy, vast.
part. (f. -a) = vi-stīrṇ, q.v.
s.f. Extensiveness; expandedness; diffusedness; largeness; amplitude.
s.f. Spreading; extension, expansion; extent; width, breadth; — diameter (of a circle).
s.f. Cholera morbus, or the first stage of it.
adj. (f. -ā), Completely dried up, very dry; withered; thirsty.
s.f. Name of a daughter of Daksh and wife of Dharm; — the plant Aconitum ferox (syn. ati-vishā); — name of a tree used for dyeing, Betula (syn. atis).

adj. & s.m. All; every; entire, whole, universal; — the whole world, the universe, all creation; — name of a kind of deity belonging to a class of ten (according to the Vishṇu and other Purāṇs they were sons of Viśvā, daughter of Daksh, and their names are given as follow, Vasu, Satya, Kratu, Daksh, Kāl, Kām, Dhriti, Kuru, Purū-rav, and Mādrav; they are particularly worshipped at the funeral obsequies in honour of deceased ancestors, and receive oblations of ghī at the daily domestic śrāddh); — a symbol for the number thirteen; — dry ginger
subs: (˚va+at˚), s.m. The Soul of the Universe, the Universal Spirit, Supreme Being; — an epithet of Brahmā, of Śiva, and of Vishṇu

(˚va+am˚, or viśvā = viśva), s.m. Name of a celebrated Kshatriya, deriving his lineage from an ancestor of Kuśik of the lunar race; he was king of Kanyā-kubjā or Kanauj (his fame rests chiefly on his contests with the great Brāhmaṇ Vasishṭh, and his success in elevating himself to the rank of a Brāhmaṇ by long and painful austerities. According to the Rāmāyaṇ, he became the companion and counsellor of the young Rām-ćandra. He was the father of Śakuntala by the nymph Menakā, whom the gods, jealous of his increasing power, sent to seduce him from his passionless life)
(˚va+an˚), s.m. Name of a son of Su-shadman
adj. All-conquering, all-subduing; — s.m. Name of a particular ceremony or sacrifice; — the noose of Varuṇ
s.m. All men, all mankind, the human race
or viśva-janīya, or viśva-janya, adj. Fit, suitable, or good for all men, universally beneficial or salutary
adj. & s.m. (f. -iṇī), All-maintaining, all-sustaining; — an epithet of the Deity
s.m. A deity of the particular class called Viśva, q.v.
adj. Taking all forms, existing in all forms; of every kind; universal; omnipresent; — s.m. An epithet of Vishṇu
s.m. The esculent root Arum colocasia
s.m. The prickly pear, Cactus indicus (lit. 'having all essence')
or viśva-sarj, s.m. Creator of the universe, an epithet of Brahmā
vulg. viśva-sr̤ishṭā, s.m. Creator of the Universe, the Supreme Being
or vulg. viśva-sukhad, adj. (f. -ā), Imparting happiness to the universe; — an epithet of Rām as the source of universal happiness
s.m. Creator or maker of all things, the Supreme Being; — an epithet of Viśva-karmā
s.m. Name of the architect and artist of the gods (said to be son of Brahmā); — an epithet of the Sun; — any great saint (cf. biswa-karmā)
adj. & s.m. Supporting the universe; all-sustaining; — the Supreme Being; — an epithet of Vishṇu, and of Indra
s.m. 'Lord of the universe,' name of Śiva (esp. as the object of adoration at Benares)
adj. & s.m. Whose dwelling-place is the universe; universally situated; all-pervading; — dwelling in or occupying the universe; — an epithet of Vishṇu
or viśva-vyāpī, adj. Permeating all things, all-pervading, all-diffused; ubiquitous; omnipresent
s.f. Universal diffusion or permeation; ubiquity; omnipresence.

def: s.m. = H وسوي विस्वी visvī, adj. corr. of viśva, and viśvī, qq.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Tasteless, flavourless, insipid; — s.m. Tastelessness, insipidity.
s.f. A sort of gull, Larus ridibundus.
s.m. For this and other compounds of which viśva is the first member, see s.v. وشوه viśva.
s.m. See s.v. وشوه viśva.
adj. Discordant, inharmonious, unmusical.
s.m. corr. of وشواس viśvās, q.v. (and see biswās).
s.m. Trust, confidence, reliance, faith, belief (i.q. biswās)
subs: (˚sa+arha), adj. (f. -ā), Worthy of trust, &c. (i.q. viśvās-yogya, q.v.)
s.m. Breach of faith; violation of confidence, &c. (i.q. viśvās-ghāt)
s.m. (f. -ī), One in whom confidence is placed, a confidential agent, a trustworthy person, a faithful one
(with loc.), or viśvās rakhnā, or viśvās karnā (-kā, or -par), See biswās rakhnā, s.v. biswās
adj. & s.m. Inspiring confidence, causing trust or faith; — one who inspires confidence or trust, &c., a confidant
s.m., or viśvās-ghāti or ghāt, s.f. See biswās-ghāt
or viśvās-ghātī, adj. & s.m. Destroying confidence; violating or betraying confidence, treacherous, perfidious; — one who betrays confidence, a traitor, treacherous friend (i.q. biswās-ghātī)
or viśvās-yog, vulg. biswās-jog, adj. Worthy of confidence, or belief, &c.; trustworthy, credible.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit विश्वास (viśvāsá). First attested as Old Hindi विश्वास (viśvāsa). Doublet of بِسْواس (bisvās).

def: adj. & s.m. = viśvāsī, q.v.
s.m. Trusting, confiding in.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), See biswāsī.
part. (f. -ā), Trusted, confided in, relied on; worthy of trust, deserving confidence; trusty; faithful; — full of confidence, bold, fearless; unsuspecting.
s.f. A widow.
s.f. = S وشوستتو विश्वस्तत्व vi-śvastatva, s.m. Trustworthiness; faithfulness; boldness, fearlessness.
s.m. = S وشوستتا विश्वस्तता vi-śvastatā, s.f. Trustworthiness; faithfulness; boldness, fearlessness.
part. adj. To be trusted or confided in, trustworthy; to be believed, credible (i.q. viśvās-yogya).
s.m. See s.v. وشوه viśva.
adj. = H وسوه विस्व visva, s.m. corr. of viśva, and viśvī, qq.v.
s.f. Name of a river (said to be situated in the Panjāb).
s.m. The second in order of the seven lower regions or regions under the earth (cf. pātāl)
subs: s.m. The world or lower region of Vital.

def: adj. Empty, vacant; pithless, sapless; dull, stupid; — wicked, abandoned; — s.m. Stretching, spreading out; expansion; — an awning, a canopy, cover; a cushion; — warp (of cloth); — an oblation, a sacrifice; the sacrificial hearth (on which the sacred fire is kept); — a heap, quantity; — season, opportunity.
v.t. prop. بتانا bitānā, q.v.

s.f. Cavil, captious objection, hypercriticism, perverse or frivolous argument, frivolous or fallacious controversy or wrangling; controversy, debate; criticism; — the esculent root Arum colocasia; — the oleander plant, Nerium odorum; — a ladle, spoon.

s.m. The young branch of a tree or creeper; a new shoot, a sprout; a branch; — a bush; a clump, cluster, thicket, tuft; — spreading, expansion; — the septum of the scrotum; — (S. viṭa+pa), a keeper of catamites.
s.m. A crossing or passing over; — quitting, giving up, abandoning; — gift, donation, distribution of charity.

s.f. A raised square piece of ground in the centre of a house or temple; a kind of covered terrace in the middle of a courtyard; a quadrangular seat or bench made of wood, a place in a courtyard for sitting in or standing under; a kind of altar or temporary stage or shed in the middle of a courtyard (for performing sacrifices, &c.); a covered temporary platform on which idols are sometimes placed; a verandah, a balcony.

s.m. Reasoning, argument, inference; conjecture, guess, supposition, opinion, anticipation; discussion; deliberation, consideration of probabilities or alternatives; doubt, apprehension; — an instructor in divine knowledge.
s.m. Reasoning; inferring, conjecturing; argument, discussion; reflection; doubt.
s.f. Name of a river in the Panjāb, the modern Jhelam (the Hydaspes of the Greeks).
s.f. A long span measured by the extended thumb and little finger (considered equal to the breadth of twelve fingers; — cf. bilast).
part. Stretched, spread out, extended, expanded; lengthened, elongated; spread over or through, diffused.
s.f. = S وتتتو विततत्व vi-tatatva, s.m. Extendedness, extension, expansiveness, largeness, amplitude.
s.m. = S وتتتا विततता vi-tatatā, s.f. Extendedness, extension, expansiveness, largeness, amplitude.
adj. At variance with the fact, incorrect, untrue, false; unreal, vain, futile.
s.f. Stretching out, spreading, extension, expansion; — a collection, quantity; — a clump, cluster (of trees, &c.).
s.m. = biṭaurā, q.v.

s.f. A row, line; road; — terrace (in front of a house); — a stall, shop; — a sort of drama (or the dramatic narration of an amatory intrigue); — a particular division of the planetary sphere (comprising three asterisms).
s.f. The betel plant, Piper betel; — a preparation of the Areca nut with spices and chunam enveloped in a leaf of the betel plant; pān (see bīṛī, the pop. form).

ety: witrār˚, prob. = S. वितारय(ति), caus. of rt. वि+तॄ
def: v.t. To carry safely through or over, to rescue, save, deliver.
s.f. = next, q.v.
adj. (f. -ā), Free from thirst or desire, satisfied, content; — dissatisfied (with), disinclined (to), disliking.
s.f. = S وترشنتو वितृष्नत्व vi-tr̤ishnatva, s.m. Freedom from desire, satiety, satisfaction, contentment; — dissatisfaction, disinclination, indifference (to); disgust, aversion.
s.m. = S وترشنتا वितृष्णता vi-tr̤ishṇatā, s.f. Freedom from desire, satiety, satisfaction, contentment; — dissatisfaction, disinclination, indifference (to); disgust, aversion.
s.f. A sort of thrush (usually, though inaccurately, called in Bengal, the Mainā).
s.m. Wealth, property, possessions, goods, money; substance, thing; — power, ability, means
subs: adj. (f. -vatī), Possessing property, wealthy, opulent, rich
adj. (f. -ā), Deprived of wealth, destitute of property, indigent, poor.

def: s.m. See the pop. form bibād.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit विवाद (vivāda). Doublet of بِباد (bibād)

def: adj. & s.m. = vi-vādī, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), See the pop. form bibādī.
s.m. (old H.) = vivāh, q.v.
s.m. Bringing home a wife, taking a wife, marriage; a wedding, nuptial form (see bibāh, and byāh, pop. forms).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī) = vi-vāhit, and byāhā, qq.v.
